text.title: Prototyping Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Prototyping. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each prototype loop tests exactly ONE falsifiable hypothesis; multi-hypothesis sprints are split.
rule.rationale: Multi-hypothesis sprints produce ambiguous outcomes; one hypothesis per loop keeps signal clean.

rule.statement: Variant is the smallest possible artefact that can test the hypothesis; over-built prototypes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Over-built prototypes drift toward production and slow the loop; smallest viable keeps iteration fast.

rule.statement: Outcome is decided by evidence (5-user test / metric / structured review); opinion-only outcomes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Opinion-only outcomes produce folklore; evidence-driven outcomes are auditable.

rule.statement: Outcome is one of (kept | rolled-back | shipped-with-changes); ambiguous 'partial' is split into two iterations.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous outcomes hide regret; trinary forces a clear next step.

rule.statement: Every iteration produces a row via prototype-iteration-log-template; unlogged iterations are folklore.
rule.rationale: Without logs, iterations vanish; the log row creates the audit trail.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-prototyping.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
